from __future__ import annotations

import unittest

from src.trader.execution.opportunity_candidate import (
    OpportunityCandidate,
    OpportunityCandidateLineage,
    OpportunityFeatureInputs,
)
from src.trader.execution.opportunity_candidate_store import (
    OpportunityCandidateStoreRecord,
)
from src.trader.execution.scanner_snapshot import (
    ScannerSnapshotError,
    build_scanner_snapshot,
    build_scanner_snapshot_from_store_records,
    build_scanner_snapshot_metadata,
    normalize_scanner_snapshot,
)


class TestScannerSnapshot(unittest.TestCase):
    def _candidate(self, suffix: str, chain: str, scanner_run_id: str, observed_at_utc: str) -> OpportunityCandidate:
        return OpportunityCandidate(
            chain=chain,
            venue="jupiter",
            instrument=f"{chain.upper()}/USDC",
            symbol=chain.upper(),
            pair=f"{chain.upper()}/USDC",
            side="LONG",
            opportunity_kind="momentum",
            observed_at_utc=observed_at_utc,
            freshness_bucket="fresh",
            lineage=OpportunityCandidateLineage(
                scanner_run_id=scanner_run_id,
                discovery_source_id=f"scanner_family_{suffix}",
                source_family="scanner",
                source_opportunity_id=f"source_opportunity_{suffix}",
                strategy_hint_id=f"strategy_hint_{suffix}",
            ),
            feature_inputs=OpportunityFeatureInputs(
                route_score=0.9,
                liquidity_score=0.8,
                freshness_score=0.95,
                latency_ms=40.0,
                expected_edge_bps=20.0,
                market_structure_score=0.6,
                spread_bps=3.0,
            ),
            tags=["scanner", "phase4"],
            metadata={"suffix": suffix},
        )

    def _store_record(self, candidate: OpportunityCandidate, appended_at_utc: str) -> OpportunityCandidateStoreRecord:
        return OpportunityCandidateStoreRecord(
            opportunity_candidate_id=candidate.opportunity_candidate_id,
            scanner_run_id=candidate.lineage.scanner_run_id,
            appended_at_utc=appended_at_utc,
            candidate=candidate,
            metadata={},
        )

    def test_round_trip_and_stable_identity(self) -> None:
        candidates = [
            self._candidate("001", "solana", "scanner_run_001", "2026-04-03T15:00:00Z"),
            self._candidate("002", "ethereum", "scanner_run_001", "2026-04-03T15:05:00Z"),
        ]
        snapshot = build_scanner_snapshot(
            scanner_run_id="scanner_run_001",
            candidates=candidates,
            tags=["snapshot", "phase4"],
            metadata={"patch": 174},
        )
        restored = normalize_scanner_snapshot(snapshot.to_dict())

        self.assertEqual(snapshot.scanner_snapshot_id, restored.scanner_snapshot_id)
        self.assertEqual(restored.metadata["patch"], 174)
        self.assertEqual(restored.candidate_count, 2)

    def test_build_from_store_records_preserves_counts_and_chains(self) -> None:
        candidates = [
            self._candidate("010", "solana", "scanner_run_shared", "2026-04-03T15:10:00Z"),
            self._candidate("011", "solana", "scanner_run_shared", "2026-04-03T15:11:00Z"),
            self._candidate("012", "ethereum", "scanner_run_shared", "2026-04-03T15:12:00Z"),
        ]
        store_records = [
            self._store_record(candidates[0], "2026-04-03T15:10:10Z"),
            self._store_record(candidates[1], "2026-04-03T15:11:10Z"),
            self._store_record(candidates[2], "2026-04-03T15:12:10Z"),
        ]

        snapshot = build_scanner_snapshot_from_store_records(
            scanner_run_id="scanner_run_shared",
            store_records=store_records,
        )
        metadata = build_scanner_snapshot_metadata(snapshot)

        self.assertEqual(snapshot.candidate_count, 3)
        self.assertEqual(metadata["candidate_count"], 3)
        self.assertEqual(len(snapshot.chain_summaries), 2)
        self.assertEqual(
            {summary.chain: summary.candidate_count for summary in snapshot.chain_summaries},
            {"ethereum": 1, "solana": 2},
        )

    def test_empty_candidates_fail_closed(self) -> None:
        with self.assertRaises(ScannerSnapshotError):
            build_scanner_snapshot(scanner_run_id="scanner_run_empty", candidates=[])

    def test_mixed_scanner_run_candidates_fail_closed(self) -> None:
        candidates = [
            self._candidate("100", "solana", "scanner_run_a", "2026-04-03T16:00:00Z"),
            self._candidate("101", "ethereum", "scanner_run_b", "2026-04-03T16:01:00Z"),
        ]

        with self.assertRaises(ScannerSnapshotError):
            build_scanner_snapshot(scanner_run_id="scanner_run_a", candidates=candidates)

    def test_mixed_store_record_scanner_runs_fail_closed(self) -> None:
        candidate_a = self._candidate("200", "solana", "scanner_run_a", "2026-04-03T17:00:00Z")
        candidate_b = self._candidate("201", "ethereum", "scanner_run_b", "2026-04-03T17:01:00Z")
        store_records = [
            self._store_record(candidate_a, "2026-04-03T17:00:10Z"),
            self._store_record(candidate_b, "2026-04-03T17:01:10Z"),
        ]

        with self.assertRaises(ScannerSnapshotError):
            build_scanner_snapshot_from_store_records(
                scanner_run_id="scanner_run_a",
                store_records=store_records,
            )

    def test_invalid_chain_summary_distribution_fails_closed(self) -> None:
        candidate = self._candidate("300", "solana", "scanner_run_one", "2026-04-03T18:00:00Z")
        payload = build_scanner_snapshot(
            scanner_run_id="scanner_run_one",
            candidates=[candidate],
        ).to_dict()
        payload["chain_summaries"] = [{"chain": "ethereum", "candidate_count": 1}]

        with self.assertRaises(ScannerSnapshotError):
            normalize_scanner_snapshot(payload)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

# Scanner Snapshot

## Purpose

Patch 174 introduces the canonical scanner snapshot object.

Patch 172 created the canonical discovery candidate.
Patch 173 persisted discovery candidates append-only.
Patch 174 groups one scanner run into one canonical snapshot so later ranking and dashboard work can reason about a stable run-shaped object instead of loose candidate lists.

## Why this layer exists

The scanning layer now has canonical candidates and durable append-only history, but it still needs one grouping layer that answers:

- which candidates belong to one scanner run,
- how many candidates were discovered,
- which chains were represented,
- what the earliest and latest observation times were.

Without this layer, later ranking and read-model work would still reconstruct run boundaries ad hoc.

## Core objects

### `ScannerSnapshotChainSummary`
Represents the chain distribution inside one scanner run.

It preserves:

- chain
- candidate count

### `ScannerSnapshot`
Represents one canonical scanner-run snapshot.

It includes:

- scanner run id
- candidate count
- earliest observed timestamp
- latest observed timestamp
- candidate list
- derived chain summaries
- tags
- metadata
- deterministic snapshot identity

## Core functions

### `build_scanner_snapshot`
Builds a snapshot directly from canonical candidates.

### `build_scanner_snapshot_from_store_records`
Builds a snapshot from append-only candidate store records.

### `build_scanner_snapshot_metadata`
Creates a light metadata/read-model surface from the canonical snapshot.

## Conservative rules

This layer remains fail-closed.

Examples:

- empty snapshots fail,
- mixed scanner run ids fail,
- mismatched chain summaries fail,
- malformed candidate inputs fail.

## What this layer does not do

This patch does not:

- rank opportunities,
- allocate capital,
- submit execution work,
- widen live behavior.

It only groups one scanner run into one canonical snapshot.

## Interaction with nearby patches

- Patch 172 defines the canonical discovery candidate.
- Patch 173 persists discovery candidates append-only.
- Patch 174 groups candidates into canonical scanner snapshots.
- Patch 175 should normalize the richer ranking feature pack.

## Validation surface

The patch should prove at least these behaviors:

- round-trip serialization is stable,
- building from store records preserves counts and chain distribution,
- empty candidates fail closed,
- mixed scanner run candidates fail closed,
- mixed store-record scanner runs fail closed,
- invalid chain summary distributions fail closed.

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

$projectRoot = "C:\dev\trader"
$venvPython = Join-Path $projectRoot ".venv\Scripts\python.exe"

if (-not (Test-Path $venvPython)) {
    throw "Expected venv python not found: $venvPython"
}

& $venvPython -m unittest tests.test_scanner_snapshot -v

notes: Patch 174 all-in-one PatchOps runner with inline authored content.
